intro: Turanga bonnetiana, also known as Turanga, is a plant species native to New Caledonia, belonging to the family Annonaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Turanga bonnetiana include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Turanga is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, particularly those related to the respiratory and gastrointestinal systems.
